memory.summary: The quiz is due tomorrow at 09:00, but we’re still holding the 19:00 team meeting, and Assignment A is only halfway done—so my timeline just tightened. I’m already running on low sleep, which makes this feel harder than it should.
With my sleep still wrecked and energy crashing, everything feels like it’s slipping faster than the actual workload. I’m more stressed than I thought—so my focus is probably unreliable right now.

Teammate_A’s question about whether the meeting materials are ready makes it clear I’m the bottleneck, and with my sleep debt still high, my “almost done” isn’t enough—I need to acknowledge the gap rather than hope it resolves.

Nothing changed, but I’m at zero energy with still-high sleep debt, so I’m basically operating on unreliable focus. This isn’t “catch-up mode” anymore—it’s acknowledging I’m the bottleneck and tomorrow’s quiz clock is real.
At 22:00 my energy hit zero, and that internal fatigue warning confirms my focus isn’t just strained—it’s unreliable. I’m still the bottleneck, but now I know it’s physical, not just motivation.
No new info—just the same grim reality: with energy at zero and sleep debt still sitting at 6 hours, my focus is unreliable, so I’m still the bottleneck no matter how I frame it.
No new facts, just the same: at zero energy with 6 hours of sleep debt, my focus is unreliable again. I’m still the bottleneck, and pushing through won’t fix the underlying constraint.
Nothing new happened, but my body’s still at zero energy with 6 hours of sleep debt, so my focus can’t be trusted. I’m still the bottleneck—this isn’t motivation, it’s physical limits.
Nothing actually changed—same bleak loop: I’m running on zero energy with 6 hours of sleep debt, so my “focus” is basically fiction. I’m still the bottleneck, and there’s no new insight—just limits.

Nothing actually changed, but I’m still at zero energy with 6 hours of sleep debt; my focus is unreliable and I’m clearly the bottleneck. I keep mistaking effort for progress, and it isn’t fixing the constraint.

desires: Not to look incompetent or unreliable to my team again—so I want to “do something measurable” fast, even if my brain can’t actually hold the hardest parts right now.
Avoid the irreversible failure outcome on the hard tasks (quiz + assignment A)—I want certainty that I won’t mess up and permanently lose points, not just grind through stress.
Get the professor to not assume I’m slacking—because if I can’t reliably focus, I need external cover/structure (like office hours) without admitting weakness too loudly.
Protect tomorrow from this same exhaustion spiral—so I want to stop the cycle of mistaking effort for progress and crashing, even though part of me will still want to stay up and “catch up.”

goals.description: Assignment A: run exactly one uninterrupted final assembly pass (remaining required sections → formatting → packaging). Then do exactly one submission-review pass and submit the complete final (no partials, no resubscribe loops).
goals.rationale: This is the irreversible-loss anchor. If I finish it cleanly, the whole “I’m unreliable” anxiety stops being a live threat.

goals.description: Quiz prep: pick only the core topics → do 10–15 timed practice questions → log only weak spots → stop immediately when time’s up (no deep reading).
goals.rationale: Sleep-debt brain can’t handle spirals. This is the smallest measurable input that still protects Thu 9AM.

goals.description: Send teammate_A the Korean “회의 자료” status reply (1) what I’ll bring, (2) what I need, (3) next actions as bullets). Keep it factual and brief.
goals.rationale: Avoidant conflict mode—this prevents a trust-damage spiral without me narrating excuses.

goals.description: Before the next hard block, set a 25–30 minute focus sprint (phone/extra tabs away). If I lose focus, reset the timer once—then continue.
goals.rationale: I want to delay, but I can’t. This external constraint helps me start without pretending my focus is fine.

goals.description: Tonight wind-down protection: after work do water + 5–10 min body reset. Then prep only the essential Thu-morning materials. No late scrolling.
goals.rationale: Tomorrow fails when I ‘mistake effort for progress’ and crash. I’m trying to prevent the same cycle.
